content.summaryText: DASH Diet in Practice in Clinical Nutrition in Chronic Disease focuses on condition-specific nutrition adjustment and adherence. Primary objective: Select sodium-smart options.

content.questionText: In DASH Diet in Practice, which action best supports 'Select sodium-smart options'?
content.explanation: Correct answers prioritize patient safety, reassessment, and timely escalation.

options.text: Align meal strategy with diagnosis, meds, and lab priorities
Applying one diet template to all chronic disease profiles
Delay reassessment until symptoms are severe
Use treatment decisions without baseline documentation

content.summaryText: Immediate priority: align meal strategy with diagnosis, meds, and lab priorities. Practical emphasis: Increase potassium-rich foods.

content.questionText: Escalation is required when poor control despite adherence, renal risk markers, or recurrent admissions.
content.explanation: Early escalation reduces preventable deterioration and delays in definitive care.

content.sentence:
Start with <1> to establish baseline status.
Use <2> to deliver standardized intervention.
Document <3> to support continuity and quality review.
